title: Roasted Vietnamese Egg Yolk Noodles
description: A malty, fluffy, light Vietnamese noodle dish featuring roasted noodles tossed in a smoky egg yolk sauce, perfect for a Mediterranean-friendly meal.

ingredients.name: tamari or low sodium soy sauce

instructions: Preheat oven to 200°C and lightly oil a baking sheet.
Combine vegetable broth, tamari, miso, sugar, salt, smoked paprika, garlic, ginger, and olive oil in a small saucepan and bring to a simmer for 2 minutes.
Place the dried noodles on the sheet and drizzle 1 tablespoon of the broth mixture over them, ensuring even coating.
Roast noodles in the oven for 12 minutes, flipping halfway, until they are golden and slightly crisp.
Whisk the egg yolks together until smooth; set aside.
Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of broth mixture in a nonstick skillet over medium heat, then add the whisked yolks, stirring constantly until they thicken into a creamy sauce.
Toss the roasted noodles with the creamy yolk sauce, mixing until fully coated.
Transfer noodles to a serving bowl, and garnish with chopped cilantro, lime wedges, and roasted peanuts if desired.
Serve immediately as a light lunch, with extra lime for acidity.
Store leftovers in an airtight container for up to 2 days.
